title: 1 dead after car crashes into tree in Chelmsford
author: John Waller
bullets: A man died after crashing his car into a tree at 75 Littleton Road in Chelmsford, Massachusetts.
The accident occurred around 12:30 p.m. on Tuesday, with the driver succumbing to his injuries at a local hospital.
Authorities are investigating the crash, and the victim's identity has not been released as of Wednesday morning.

title: Local obituary: Janet Swallow, 68, Danvers resident and longtime Lahey nurse
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Janet M. Swallow, 68, a Danvers resident and longtime Lahey nurse, died at her home on March 12, 2026.
Swallow worked over 30 years at Lahey Medical Center in Burlington in Critical Care and Intensive Care Units.
She is survived by two sons, a granddaughter, her sister and brother, and her stepson and two friends.

title: A decade after her father’s death, 24-year-old runs the Boston Marathon for grief support
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Violet Morgan is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her father, who died of colorectal cancer in 2016.
Morgan is partnering with The Children's Room, supporting families who have experienced the death of a parent or sibling.
Despite disliking running, Morgan found inspiration in the Boston Marathon and now uses it to honor her father.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 18)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Boston condo at 89 Beacon St. sold for $17 million, the highest sale in the area.
Four properties in Acton, including a townhouse on Railroad St., ranged from $314,159 to $1.2 million.
A two-family property on Upland Road in Belmont sold for $1,372,000 while a Colonial in Abington sold for $460,000.

title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play ranks 28th in the NHL with a 12.8% success rate since the Olympic break.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have not produced any points in 5-on-5 play over the last 11 games.
Defenseman Andrew Peeke continues to rotate with Henri Jokiharju due to inconsistent performance on the third defensive pairing.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit crucial home runs for Venezuela, contributing to their first-ever World Baseball Classic title victory.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez delivered four scoreless innings in the championship, allowing one hit and striking out four.
Garrett Whitlock allowed the winning run, and Roman Anthony struck out to end the game as Team USA fell to Venezuela 3-2.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan, 39, from Ashland, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
Flanagan started running three years ago for exercise, improving her mental health and embracing the challenge of the Boston Marathon.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center supports children with complex medical needs and provides relief for their families.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the death of their 10-month-old foster child.
The child died from complications of a strep infection and bronchopneumonia, which were treatable with timely medical care.
The couple faces up to 20 years in prison for manslaughter and 2.5 years for reckless child endangerment; sentencing is March 19.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A major water main break in Jackson Square flooded streets and disrupted traffic Tuesday afternoon in Jamaica Plain.
The flooding prompted the MBTA to detour bus routes 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44 and temporarily close the Jackson Square busway.
Crews were working to shut off the 36-inch water main by evening, affecting a vast area beyond Egleston Square.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Denver Broncos acquired Jaylen Waddle from Miami Dolphins for a first-round pick and two additional draft picks.
New England Patriots may need a first-round pick to trade for Eagles' A.J. Brown after Broncos' deal for Waddle.
The Waddle trade improves Broncos, a key Patriots competitor, by pairing him with quarterback Bo Nix and receiver Courtland Sutton.
